compute.cost_note: spaCy en_core_web_sm dependency parse; CPU, no GPU/LLM.

do_not_use_when: You have no writer-specific baseline (the audit refuses to run on a single document)
You want an absolute "too smooth / too rough" verdict or a quality score (this surface refuses it)
You want a voice-identity, authorship, or AI-provenance call

artifacts: stdout markdown report (default), --json envelope, or --out PATH

purpose: Strictly baseline-relative productive-roughness deviation profile — fragment rate, sentence-initial coordinating-conjunction rate, contraction rate, adjacent-word-repetition rate, interjection/aside rate, very-short- sentence rate. Reports each as the draft's deviation (draft, baseline mean ± sd, z) from the WRITER'S OWN baseline, never an absolute roughness or quality judgment.
use_when: You want to see how a draft's sentence-internal roughness deviates from a writer's own stable pattern
You have a writer-specific baseline corpus (their prior work) to compare against
You suspect AI editing has sanded off (or amplified) a writer's productive roughness
